content: te the system, not just watch it. So I added 10 control plane tools to the same MCP server. Three safety tiers built on MCP's existing primitives. Read freely. Mutate with confirmation. Destructive ops need a dry-run first. Slide 6 has the full breakdown. What this leads to: observe, hypothesize, change a config value via hot-reload, measure. All in one conversation. The product becomes a lab bench. The agent is both the experimenter and the instrument (slide 7). I'm still early. The tools are shipped but the workflows around them are still forming. MCP standardized how agents talk to tools. There's no standard yet for how agents operate the systems they talk to. Someone needs to make that plug-and-play. What's the most interesting thing you've let an AI agent change in a running system? Or are you still keeping the keys? #OpenTelemetry #MCP #AIAgents #Observability #Rust
